title: epipactis latifolia subsp. rubiginosa
intro: Epipactis latifolia subsp. rubiginosa, also known as the bog helleborine, is a subspecies of orchid native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its broad leaves and reddish-brown stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. It is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy. Herbal preparations of Epipactis latifolia subsp. rubiginosa include infusions and decoctions, although the plant's use is not widely recommended due to potential toxicity and the need for further study.
